title: B-Splines (Basis Splines)
body:
B-Splines (Basis Splines) are a type of curve used in computer graphics and design. They are defined by a set of basis functions $N_{i,p}(\xi)$ calculated using the **Cox-de Boor recursion formula**:

Degree $p=0$:
$N_{i,0}(\xi) = 1$ if $\xi_i \le \xi < \xi_{i+1}$, else $0$.

Degree $p > 0$:
$$N_{i,p}(\xi) =  rac{\xi - \xi_i}{\xi_{i+p} - \xi_i} N_{i,p-1}(\xi) +  rac{\xi_{i+p+1} - \xi}{\xi_{i+p+1} - \xi_{i+1}} N_{i+1,p-1}(\xi)$$

Control points act like 'magnets' to pull the curve into shape. Unlike some other types of curves, B-splines do not necessarily pass through their control points. One of the main advantages of B-splines is 'local control', meaning that moving one control point only affects a small part of the curve.
